news.title: SC sets up expert panel on Aravalis, petitioners question its independence
news.para: The Supreme Court has formed a five-member high-powered committee to redefine the 'Aravali hills and ranges', dropping the controversial 100m height criterion. This committee will assess the ecological impact of regulated mining and identify areas excluded from protection, aiming to prevent further degradation of the ancient mountain ranges.

news.title: Indore district records doubling of housing stock over 15 years
news.para: Indore district has seen a substantial rise in homes and people over the last fifteen years. The initial census phase shows housing units more than doubling. Population figures have also climbed significantly. This growth is evident in both urban and rural areas. The second phase of the census will confirm final population numbers.

news.title: Rajasthan lifts cap on building height, set to embrace skyscrapers
news.para: Rajasthan is set to witness the rise of skyscrapers, with the state government removing the 70-meter height cap on buildings. This move, following a central government regulation, will allow for vertical development of commercial towers, schools, and hospitals. Buildings will now be permitted to extend to any height, provided they meet advanced firefighting system standards as per NBCS 2026.

news.title: Rents hit new highs around Hyderabad IT hubs
news.para: Soaring house rentals in Hyderabad's IT corridor are forcing professionals into shared accommodations and longer commutes. Rents for 1BHK and 2BHK apartments have significantly increased, pushing many to seek more affordable options further away from their workplaces. This trend impacts newcomers and mid-level employees the most, creating a difficult balance between cost and convenience.

news.title: British Land names Joanne McNamara as CEO
news.para: British Land Company has appointed Joanne McNamara as its new chief executive officer, succeeding Simon Carter. McNamara, currently executive vice president of European business at Oxford Properties, brings extensive real estate expertise and a strong reputation for leadership. Her appointment comes as the company reported strong annual profits, driven by demand for office spaces from AI and tech firms.

news.title: Delhi CM directs MCD to identify unauthorised, illegal building constructions after Saket incident
news.para: Following a fatal building collapse in Saket, Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has ordered the MCD to conduct a comprehensive survey of unauthorized constructions across Delhi. The directive aims to identify irregularities and ensure strict action against those responsible for violations of safety norms and administrative negligence.

news.title: Delhi: MCD ties up with agencies to hold drone surveys to identify unassessed properties
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has partnered with four agencies for drone-based geospatial surveys to identify unassessed properties. These pilot projects will utilize advanced technologies like LiDAR and Digital Twin to create detailed digital records, aiming to bring properties into the tax net and support urban planning. Successful completion could lead to a city-wide rollout.

news.title: British mortgage approvals rise to 15-month high, Bank of England data shows
news.para: British mortgage approvals reached a 15-month high in April, exceeding economists' expectations, while consumer credit also grew faster than anticipated. This resilience in lending suggests some economic strength despite increased borrowing costs. However, analysts caution that this surge may be temporary, with a potential future decline in approvals as higher mortgage rates impact the housing market.

news.title: Delhi: MCD booked 4,766 unauthorised buildings in 2025; 22 demolished fully
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi booked 4,766 unauthorized constructions and issued 3,989 demolition orders in 2025. Action was taken against 3,294 booked properties, with 22 fully demolished, 2,249 partly demolished, and 1,023 sealed. This intensified enforcement follows a recent building collapse that killed six people.

news.title: Maharashtra government acquires Air India building in Mumbai for ₹1,601 crore
news.para: The Maharashtra government has officially acquired the iconic Air India building in Mumbai for Rs 1,601 crore. This landmark property will be transformed into a model government complex, aiming to consolidate state offices and reduce rental expenses. The Public Works Department will oversee structural audits and renovations to ensure quality and sustainability.

news.title: Madhya Pradesh cabinet gives nod to SVAMITVA scheme for grant of title deeds
news.para: Madhya Pradesh cabinet approved the 'Ownership Rights Record Execution and Registration Scheme-2026' to provide officially registered property documents to landholders without titles. This initiative aims to enable citizens to access bank loans for housing, business, and agriculture, fostering economic growth.

news.title: Jhansi greenfield industrial city gets environment clearance
news.para: The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has granted environmental clearance to the Bundelkhand Industrial Development Authority’s greenfield industrial city project in Jhansi. The project, planned across 62,599.20 acres, is expected to attract investment of about ₹1.51 lakh crore and generate employment for over 5.6 lakh people.

news.title: Welspun One plans to lease over 10 million sq ft in next three years
news.para: Welspun One plans to lease over 10 million sq ft of warehousing and industrial real estate in the next three years, nearly doubling its current operational footprint. The company is expanding into adjacent segments like in-city distribution and retail, with key projects including a large FTWZ development at Nhava Sheva and logistics parks in Pune and Bengaluru.

news.title: NGT sets up panel to probe ‘illegal brick kiln, clay mining in Balasore
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered a joint investigation into allegations of illegal brick kiln operations and unauthorized brick clay mining in Balasore district. A multi-agency panel will verify the charges and recommend corrective measures. The tribunal directed the panel to submit its report within one month. Notices have been issued to state officials and private parties.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh acquires 100 hectares in Ghaziabad for industrial hub
news.para: Ghaziabad administration has acquired approximately 100 hectares of land in Modinagar's Niwari for a new industrial hub. Farmers received compensation at four times the circle rate, marking a significant step in boosting industrial infrastructure and attracting investment in the NCR region. This development addresses the long-standing demand for land for new industries in Ghaziabad.

news.title: How ULCRA held back Kolkata's growth and why its repeal could be a turning point for Bengal
news.para: Kolkata's urban growth faced decades of constraint due to the Urban Land (Ceiling and Regulation) Act, 1976. This law hindered large-scale development and investment. Now, the state government is considering its repeal. This move promises to unlock opportunities for real estate and infrastructure. It could simplify land acquisition and boost economic growth across West Bengal.

news.title: Chandigarh civic body nets record ₹61 crore property tax in rebate window
news.para: Chandigarh's municipal corporation achieved a record Rs 61 crore in property tax during the April-May rebate period, surpassing last year's collection. Commercial properties contributed significantly, with residential units also showing strong participation. Taxpayers who missed the deadline now face a 25% penalty and 12% interest.
